levels.name: IRON GATE
levels.desc: Snow falls steady against iron. The gate looms ahead — old metal gone gray with frost, chains thick as your arm. You've been walking through cold you can't name, following a path you can't explain. Your paws are numb. Your breath clouds the air in front of you. You don't know why you're here. That's the honest truth. Something pulled you toward this road — not hunger, not anger, not revenge. Just... forward. Like your feet knew something your head didn't. And now you're standing at a gate that feels like it should mean something, guarded by two ferrets who look like they've seen a thousand foxes come and go. One of them snorts. 'Hah — a fox? On the Path of the Sumurai? Go home, kit.' The other one doesn't even laugh. He just looks at you — really looks — like he's waiting to see what you'll do next. The snow keeps falling. The gate doesn't move. And somewhere underneath the doubt, underneath the cold, there's a quiet thing that refuses to turn around. You came this far. Might as well see what's on the other side.
levels.defeat_tip: Hestiation is defeat! Attack!
levels.clear_desc: The ferrets go quiet. One of them laughs despite himself — low, surprised, almost respectful. 'Not bad, fox.' Snow settles on all three of you. You're breathing hard. Your muscles burn from the cold and the effort. But you're standing, and they're not — and that means something, even if you don't know what yet. Then you notice it: the way they moved. Not fast, exactly. Efficient. Every step placed just so, no wasted motion, like they'd already decided where to be before they got there. Watching them weave and sidestep, something clicks. Quick Steps. You're going to need that. The gate groans open behind them. Wind cuts through, sharp and clean. Beyond it, the path stretches into white nothing — trees, shadows, the promise of more fights, more questions, more snow. You step forward. You still don't know why you're here. But you're not turning back.

levels.name: BAMBOO TRAIL
levels.desc: Boars and rabbits hold the trail. The boars are slow — but the rabbits won't stay still long enough to hit.
levels.defeat_tip: Rabbits are extremely evasive. Don't waste heavy strikes on them — wait for your opening.
levels.clear_desc: The trail is yours. The rabbits were slippery, but you found the gaps. You're starting to understand — speed isn't just about hitting first.

levels.name: FOX DEN
levels.desc: Eight rabbits have made themselves at home in the old fox den. They see you coming — and not one of them runs.
levels.defeat_tip: The rabbits move as a pack — they always strike at the 3rd turn, first action. Let them think they have you. Then make it count.
levels.clear_desc: Eight against one, and not one of them ran. When the last rabbit dropped, you heard slow clapping from the tree line. A turtle steps out of the shadows, arms folded. 'You have skill, fox,' he says. 'Let me show you something.'

levels.name: SILENT SHRINE
levels.desc: The turtle leads you to a weathered shrine at the edge of the forest. Two otters and a ferret wait inside, arms crossed. 'My students,' the turtle says. 'If you can move them, perhaps you are ready to learn what I came here to teach.'
levels.defeat_tip: The turtle is the real threat — Still Wind builds Battle Spirit every moment he's left alone. Break his concentration and it wears off. The otters are consistent: Stream Slash, every turn. Don't count on evading your way through them.
levels.clear_desc: The shrine goes still. The turtle watches the last of his students hit the ground, then exhales slowly. 'Good.' He settles into a low stance — and you see something shift. A faint pulse gathering around him, born not from movement, but from stillness. 'That,' he says, 'is Still Wind. Battle Spirit from the breath, not the blade.' He holds it a moment, then lets it go. 'You will need it before this path is done.'

levels.name: 2 Turtles and a Rabbit
levels.desc: The path ends here — for now. Two turtle masters stand side by side, a rabbit stationed between them. They've been watching you since the shrine. This is their answer.
levels.defeat_tip: This is the hardest fight on the path. Don't rush it. You've seen Still Wind — you know their rhythm. Their patience is their strength, but patience cuts both ways. Find your moment and strike when they breathe.
levels.clear_desc: The last turtle staggers back. The students watching from the ridge go silent. The elder turtle looks at you for a long moment — then bows, just slightly. 'We have not seen a fox fight like that,' he says. The students exchange glances. No one argues.

levels.name: THE FALCON'S EYE
levels.desc: A shape drops from the treetops and lands before you — a falcon, arms folded, eyes sharp. 'I've been watching since the gate,' he says. 'Not bad for a fox. Let's see if you can keep up.'
levels.defeat_tip: The falcon moves fast — faster than anything you've faced. He acts before you can answer. Watch his rhythm and don't give him room to breathe.
levels.clear_desc: The falcon tilts his head — then grins. 'Hah. You actually kept up.' He steps forward and shows you something. One motion, fluid and nearly invisible. 'Shinsoku,' he says. 'Speed your enemy can't answer. Strike before they can think.' He steps aside. 'That's yours now, fox.'

levels.name: FALCON AMBUSH
levels.desc: You don't see them coming. A blur of wings, and a falcon drops from above. Then another. Then three more. They don't look surprised to find you here. They look like they were waiting.
levels.defeat_tip: These falcons are fast — but so are you. Shinsoku exists for this moment. Strike before they can set their footing and the numbers won't matter.
levels.clear_desc: Six falcons on the ground. You stand over all of them, breathing slow. The wind moves through the trees. Somewhere ahead, the Path of the Sumurai continues. You take a step forward.

levels.name: AGAINST THE TIDE
levels.desc: A group of otters blocks the river crossing, laughing and splashing. They see you coming and fall into formation — still grinning. 'A fox on the path?' one calls out. 'Let's see what you've got!'
levels.defeat_tip: Stream Slash rides the current — you can dodge it, but you can't dodge forever. Stop running and fight back. Strike against the tide.
levels.clear_desc: The lead otter splashes over, grinning ear to ear. 'Not bad, fox! Not bad at all!' He shows you the motion — a flowing cut that follows like water. 'Stream Slash,' he says. 'Cuts right through the slippery ones. Doesn't matter how fast they move.' He winks. 'Now you try.'

levels.name: THE GREAT DEBATE
levels.desc: You hear the argument before you see them. A falcon and a turtle are standing in the middle of the path, completely blocking it, deep in what appears to be a very serious disagreement. 'Speed,' the falcon is saying. 'Speed is everything.' The turtle shakes his head slowly. 'Endurance. Outlast everything.' They both stop when they see you. A long pause. Then the falcon says, '...settle this for us?' The turtle cracks his knuckles. 'Yes. Fight us both. Whoever you struggle with more — that one wins.' This is not how you expected this conversation to go.
levels.defeat_tip: The falcon hides behind Quick Steps — Stream Slash cuts right through his dodge, no matter how fast he moves. Stack Shinsoku first to push your own Quick Steps ahead of his, then hit him before he can set it up.
levels.clear_desc: The falcon and the turtle are both on the ground. There is a long silence. The falcon speaks first. 'You were faster than me.' The turtle nods gravely. 'And you outlasted me.' Another silence. They look at each other. Then they look at you. 'So...' the falcon starts. The turtle finishes: '...a fox is better?' You shrug. You don't make the rules. The falcon groans. The turtle puts his face in his hands. Somehow you have made this worse.

levels.name: MEET THE BEAR
levels.desc: A massive bear sumurai blocks the path, arms folded. You study his stance — thick, grounded, immovable. Something about him says he isn't going anywhere. You realize with some discomfort that you two have that in common.
levels.defeat_tip: The bear hits hard and doesn't flinch. Mend your wounds before the second wave — you'll need to be standing when the boars arrive.
levels.clear_desc: The bear goes down on one knee, then looks up. He says nothing for a long moment. Then, slowly, he demonstrates it — a deliberate, focused stillness, and the wound on his arm closes. 'Mend,' he says. 'Because a warrior who cannot endure cannot protect.' He rises. 'Now you know why bears are necessary.'

levels.name: THE TIGER'S EYE
levels.desc: A tiger sumurai steps into the path ahead, lightning crackling at his fingertips. He looks you over — slow, measuring. 'A fox,' he says. Not a question. He smiles. 'Let's see what you've got.'
levels.defeat_tip: The tiger moves with the storm — fast and unpredictable. Survive the first round and read his rhythm. The rabbits in the second wave keep him covered. Don't let them.
levels.clear_desc: The tiger stands there breathing hard, then breaks into a grin. 'Ha — a fox who can keep up with lightning.' He reaches out and taps two fingers against your chest. A current runs through you, quick and sharp. 'Storm Strike,' he says. 'Strike with the storm behind it and they won't see it coming. You've earned it, fox.'

levels.name: THE RABBLE
levels.desc: They come from everywhere at once — rabbits, boars, ferrets, a wall of bodies moving fast. Then you spot it: lightning, flashing from somewhere deep in the crowd. Tigers, mixed in. Waiting.
levels.defeat_tip: Don't get tunnel vision on the small ones — there are tigers buried in the ranks. If you lose track of them, they'll tear you apart while you're busy with the rabble.
levels.clear_desc: The last ferret hits the ground. You stand in the middle of fifteen downed enemies, breathing slow. The path ahead is quiet. You earned that quiet.

levels.name: THE WOLF'S GREETING
levels.desc: A wolf sumurai steps from the treeline, frost curling off his blade. He tilts his head. 'A fox on the Path of the Sumurai?' He looks almost amused. 'Well. Canine blood is canine blood. Let's see if yours runs hot.'
levels.defeat_tip: The wolf is methodical — he doesn't rush. He waits until you overcommit and punishes it. Two wolves in the second wave will try to split your attention. Pick one and finish it fast.
levels.clear_desc: Both wolves hit the ground. The first one sits up slowly, rubbing his jaw — then laughs, low and genuine. 'Ha. Fellow canine, you fight well.' He exchanges a look with the other wolf, who gives a reluctant nod. 'We don't teach this to just anyone,' he says. He draws his blade slow and drags it through the frost — a clean, biting arc. 'Freeze Slash. Cold that lingers. Your enemies will feel it long after the strike.' He sheathes it and steps aside. 'It suits a fox.'

levels.name: TOO HARD, TOO SOFT, JUST RIGHT
levels.desc: Three bears are standing in the road, mid-argument. 'Too slow,' one says. 'Too reckless,' says another. 'Just right,' says the third, nodding at nothing in particular. You have no idea what they're discussing. A falcon is perched above the whole scene, visibly suffering. He spots you before the bears do. His expression says: please end this. You decide, on reflection, that this is a you problem now.
levels.defeat_tip: The bears only have one action — hit them with Freeze Slash and send them into an early hibernation. One less problem at a time.
levels.clear_desc: All four are on the ground. The third bear stares up at the sky. 'Was that,' he says slowly, '...just right?' You think about it. 'No,' you say. 'Definitely not just right.' A long pause. 'But was it too hard or too soft?' You walk away without answering. The falcon, still flat on the ground, gives you a silent thumbs-up. You walk a little faster.

levels.name: THE DRAGON'S WARNING
levels.desc: A dragon sumurai stands alone in the path ahead, arms folded, watching you approach without surprise. He doesn't move to stop you — he just waits. When you're close enough, he speaks. 'You've made it further than most.' A pause. 'From here, the path changes. What you've faced until now — that was the door.' He shifts his weight, and you feel the air around him tighten. 'I am what's on the other side of it. And I'm happy to show you what you'll need.'
levels.defeat_tip: The dragon reads your Battle Spirit — his aura tells you everything. Keep your Battle Spirit low and he stalls, building his own. Let it climb past 30% and he turns aggressive, hitting harder and faster. Watch the aura shift and plan around it.
levels.clear_desc: The dragon stands still for a long moment — then exhales. 'Good.' He steps toward you and demonstrates a single motion: a sharp, sweeping deflection that sheds the force of a blow before it can land. 'Harai,' he says. 'It doesn't just block — it clears. Status, pressure, momentum. One motion, and the slate is clean.' He holds the stance until you mirror it. 'You'll need that,' he says, 'where the path goes from here.'

levels.name: CHALLENGE 1
levels.desc: You have walked the Path long enough now to know the strikes, the steps, the rhythm of a fight. But somewhere past the tiger and the dragon and the long stretch of road, a question has started following you. Not about technique. About something underneath it. You don't know exactly when it started — but you've begun to wonder: what does it actually mean to be a sumurai?
levels.defeat_tip: A sumurai knows their limits. Study what overwhelmed you.
levels.clear_desc: Standing over the fallen, you feel it briefly — not pride, not relief. Something quieter. A sumurai does not ask whether they are ready. They simply meet what is in front of them. That's part of it, you think. Not all of it. But part.

levels.name: CHALLENGE 2
levels.desc: The question stayed with you longer than you expected. You turned it over between fights, between miles, in the quiet stretches where the path gives you nothing but your own thoughts. At some point you stopped waiting for an answer and just kept walking. That itself felt like something — though you couldn't say what yet.
levels.defeat_tip: A sumurai does not wait to feel ready. Keep going.
levels.clear_desc: Afterward, you realize you didn't stop to ask yourself whether you wanted to fight. You just did. Not out of anger, not out of pride — because the path required it. That, you think, is the second thing. A sumurai does not choose their moment. They answer the moment that arrives.

levels.name: CHALLENGE 3
levels.desc: There is a stillness in the ones who are most dangerous. You've noticed it — not absence of movement, but something underneath the movement. Like water that doesn't break when you hit it. You've been watching for it in yourself. You're not sure you've found it yet.
levels.defeat_tip: The blade follows the mind. Still the mind first.
levels.clear_desc: In the middle of it, for just a moment, you found it — that place where the noise goes quiet and there is only what's in front of you. It didn't last. But it was there. A sumurai carries that stillness into the fight, you think. Not after they win. Before.

levels.name: CHALLENGE 4
levels.desc: You've been beaten before. More than once. Each time, you got up. You never really thought about why — it just seemed obvious. But the path is longer now, and you're starting to wonder if getting up is the whole point. Not victory. Not perfect technique. Just: getting up.
levels.defeat_tip: A sumurai falls. What matters is what comes after.
levels.clear_desc: You hit the ground once during this fight. You got up. That's all it was — a fact, plain and unremarkable. But walking away, you understand something: the path doesn't care how many times you fall. It only asks if you're still on it. A sumurai is someone who never fully leaves the path.

levels.name: CHALLENGE 5
levels.desc: You've been carrying the question for a long time now. What is a sumurai? You've gathered pieces — showing up, stillness, getting back up. But pieces aren't an answer. You're starting to think the answer isn't something you find. It might be something you build. One fight at a time, one choice at a time, until the question stops needing to be asked.
levels.defeat_tip: You know what to do. Trust it.
levels.clear_desc: Standing at the end of it, the question is still there — but it's quieter now. Less urgent. Maybe a sumurai isn't a thing you become. Maybe it's a thing you keep deciding to be. Not once. Every day. Every road. Every fight that asks you to show up and answer. You take a breath. Keep walking.

levels.name: THE DARK FOX
levels.desc: The path ends here — or rather, it folds back on itself. Something is waiting at the edge of the road that stops you cold. Not a wolf, not a bear, not a dragon. A fox. Standing the way you stand. Breathing the way you breathe. It doesn't speak. It doesn't have to. Every question you've carried this whole road — what is a sumurai, what does it mean, what are you — has led here. To this. The answer isn't waiting at the end of the path. It was following you the whole time.
levels.defeat_tip: The dark fox is a mirror. It knows every move you know — because they are your moves. The question isn't how to beat it. The question is how well you know yourself. Do you?
levels.clear_desc: It's over. You stand looking at where the dark fox was, and there is nothing left but the road and the quiet. The question has finally gone still. A sumurai is not defined by their strikes or their footwork or the names of their techniques. A sumurai is someone who knows themselves completely — their habits, their hesitations, the patterns they fall into under pressure. You faced every version of that tonight. And you answered. That's what you are.
